mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic demand, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized but 60-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4% indicates labor market weakness, reducing consumer spending and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE remains above 4% but 5-day SPX returns exceed 1.5% in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal tightening monetary policy and inflation, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise but 20-day SPX returns exceed 1% in 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS below 2.5% indicates monetary easing, lowering discount rates and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS declines below 2.5% but 60-day SPX returns are negative in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong supply-side capacity, increasing corporate margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises > 1% but 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month surge in payroll gains (PAYEMS) above 200k signals robust labor market demand, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS exceeds 200k but 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 25% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong demand, increasing corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Both HOUST and RSAFS rise but 60-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month CPIAUCSL reading above 2.5% signals persistent inflation, prompting central bank rate hikes and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% but 5-day SPX returns exceed 1% in 60% of cases.
